channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: Family says SoCal boy died from dangerous social media challenge
item.description: A Southern California couple is mourning the death of their young son after they say he died from a dangerous social media challenge.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday, marking a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Universal reveals Shrek, SpongeBob lands for new theme park in Texas
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences recently revealed several themed areas that will be featured at Universal Kids Resort, a new family-focused theme park set to open next year in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chaotic scene unfolded as 3 were shot and killed outside Kentucky driver's license office
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Witnesses described a chaotic scene in which three people were shot and killed outside of a Kentucky driver's license office, including one man shot multiple times by masked assailants.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas frees 6 hostages but questions cloud the future of the Gaza ceasefire
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas on Saturday released the last six living hostages under the first phase of its ceasefire with Israel with a week remaining, as growing questions over the next phase clouded the future of the fragile deal.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMED JAHJOUH and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US offers UN resolution on war in Ukraine that's far short of rival European statement
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States has proposed a draft U.N. resolution that stops far short of a competing European-backed statement demanding an immediate withdrawal of all of Moscow’s forces from Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pentagon says it will cut 5,400 probationary workers starting next week
item.description: The Defense Department said Friday that it’s cutting 5,400 probationary workers starting next week and will put a hiring freeze in place. It comes after staffers from the Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE, were at the Pentagon earlier in the week and received lists of such employees, U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Diddy lawyer quits during federal sex crimes case, no reason given
item.description: Anthony Ricco notified the judge of his intent to withdraw after conversations with Diddy's lead counsel.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration reverses its previous decision and reinstates legal aid for migrant children
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Days after telling legal groups who help migrant children who arrive in America alone — some so young they are in diapers or their feet dangle from their chairs in court — that they must stop their work, the federal government Friday reversed itself.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A New Jersey man is convicted of attempted murder in the stabbing of Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A New Jersey man was convicted Friday of attempted murder for stabbing author Salman Rushdie multiple times on a New York lecture stage in 2022.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actress Hunter Schafer says she was issued a male passport
item.description: Hunter Schafer, one of Hollywood’s most visible transgender stars, said she was issued a U.S. passport with a male gender marker after her original passport, which listed her gender as female, was stolen.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dunkin' to remove upcharge for non-dairy milk: reports
item.description: Dunkin' customers will no longer be charged extra for requesting non-dairy milk in their drinks. Beginning March 5, dairy alternatives will be standard options for Dunkin' beverages at no additional cost to guests, a spokesperson from the Massachusetts-based coffee chain told multiple media outlets.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: LA mayor dismisses fire chief over response to most destructive wildfire in city history last month
item.description: Six weeks after devastating wildfires, Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass ousted the city’s fire chief amid a public rift over preparations for the fires and finger-pointing between the chief and City Hall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Blood, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SS United States to embark on final voyage to Florida
item.description: A historic ocean liner departed on Thursday for her final voyage, where it heads towards its watery grave off of Florida's coast.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, NewsNation

item.title: One-armed basketball player makes history with basket
item.description: When her high school basketball coach cut her from the team senior year, Baileigh Sinaman-Daniel refused to let that rejection, or the fact she was born with one arm, end her dream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Video shows barefoot foster child with no coat left in snow
item.description: A man in Haskell, Oklahoma, called 911 after hearing a child outside in the cold begging to go back inside a residence. Rhet Wike also captured video of the little girl standing outside in record-breaking freezing temperatures and wearing only pajamas.
item.dc:creator: Heather Holeman/KFOR, KTLA 5

item.title: The secrets to taking better smartphone photos
item.description: You don’t need a big camera to take amazing photos. Whether you’re on vacation or just capturing everyday moments, a few simple tricks—and the right apps—can make a big difference.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Video shows barefoot foster child with no coat left in snow, begging to go back inside
item.description: A man in Haskell, Oklahoma heard a child nearby begging to go back inside. When he saw the little girl standing outside in record-breaking freezing temperatures and wearing only pajamas, he called 911.
item.dc:creator: Heather Holeman/KFOR, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lost recording of MLK speech resurfaces decades later
item.description: For 30 years, a recording of Martin Luther King Jr's speech at Western Michigan University was lost. Then in 1997, it was found.
item.dc:creator: Josh Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Senate adopts budget resolution after marathon overnight vote-a-rama
item.description: The Senate adopted a budget resolution Friday intended to serve as a blueprint to deliver the first part of President Trump’s agenda. Senators voted 52-48, mostly along party lines, on the resolution after a marathon overnight voting session. Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) voted against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump backs idea to send 20% of DOGE savings to American citizens
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said that he likes the idea of giving some of the savings from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency back to U.S. citizens as a kind of dividend.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: After watching videos of a Delta Air Lines jet catch fire upon landing and flip over on a Toronto runway, it's fair to wonder how anyone could have survived.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies. State and local health departments are only just beginning to hear from officials at the CDC, nearly a month after the inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Arkansas victims of mortuary theft scheme inspire push for law banning sale of human remains
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A year had passed since Doneysha Smith suffered a stillbirth and the memories of her loss lingered in 2023.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Seven Chilean men are charged with burglarizing the homes of Mahomes, Burrow and other star athletes
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Seven men from Chile have been charged in Florida federal court with orchestrating burglaries at the homes of prominent professional athletes around the country, according to a criminal complaint.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Polar vortex makes much of US colder than Greenland, but warmth is coming. Then more cold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The polar vortex hit its peak across much of America on Wednesday, with an icy grip that made Arctic Greenland seem like a toasty vacation spot in comparison. Even Mars has been warmer than North Dakota this week. But there's hope.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, Fox 5

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multi-state killings held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Vehicle launches off overpass, plummets onto freeway below
item.description: One driver is lucky to be alive after their vehicle plummeted off an overpass in California on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9
